properties.areaDesc: Coastal Aransas; Coastal Kleberg; Coastal Nueces; Coastal San Patricio; Aransas Islands; Coastal Refugio; Coastal Calhoun; Kleberg Islands; Nueces Islands; Calhoun Islands
properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued February 27 at 3:43PM CST by NWS Corpus Christi TX
properties.description:
Patchy to areas of fog will develop this afternoon into the
overnight hours across the Coastal Bend. Expect visibilities to
generally range between 1 and 3 miles, with a few locations
experiencing visibilities 1/4 mile or less at times.

Use caution if traveling as visibility may change rapidly
in a short amount of time. Use low beam headlights and leave
extra distance between your vehicle and others on the road.

NWSheadline: PATCHY TO AREAS OF FOG THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: Teller County/Rampart Range Including Pikes Peak and Florissant Fossil Beds National Monument; Fremont County Including Canon City/Howard/Texas Creek; Southern El Paso County Including Fort Carson and Colorado Springs; Pueblo County Including Pueblo; Huerfano County Including Walsenburg; Western Las Animas County Including Trinidad and Thatcher
properties.headline: Red Flag Warning issued February 27 at 11:36AM MST until February 27 at 6:00PM MST by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
* AFFECTED AREA...Fire Weather Zones 221, 222, 227, 228, 229 and
230.

* WINDS...Northwest 10 to 20 mph with gusts up to 35 mph.

* RELATIVE HUMIDITY...5 to 10 percent.

* IMPACTS...Fires will catch and spread quickly. Exercise
extreme caution with any outdoor burning.
properties.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.

NWSheadline: RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 PM MST THIS EVENING FOR GUSTY WINDS AND LOW RELATIVE FOR FIRE WEATHER ZONES 221, 222, 227, 228, 229, AND 230

properties.areaDesc: Mobile Coastal; Baldwin Coastal; Escambia Coastal; Santa Rosa Coastal; Okaloosa Coastal
properties.headline: Rip Current Statement issued February 27 at 2:30PM CST until February 27 at 6:00PM CST by NWS Mobile AL
properties.description:
* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents.

* WHERE...In Alabama, Mobile Coastal and Baldwin Coastal
Counties. In Florida, Escambia Coastal, Santa Rosa Coastal and
Okaloosa Coastal Counties.

* WHEN...Until 6 PM CST this evening.

* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away
from shore into deeper water.
properties.instruction:
Swim near a lifeguard. If caught in a rip current, relax and
float. Don't swim against the current. If able, swim in a
direction following the shoreline. If unable to escape, face the
shore and call or wave for help.

NWSheadline: HIGH RIP CURRENT RISK REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 PM CST THIS EVENING

properties.description:
A Red Flag Warning issued by the National Weather Service in
Lincoln for wind and low relative humidity remains in effect
until 6 PM this evening.

* AFFECTED AREA...Knox, Stark, Peoria, Marshall, Woodford,
Fulton, Tazewell, Schuyler, Mason, Logan, Cass, Menard, Scott,
Morgan and Sangamon.

* TIMING...Until 6 PM CST this evening.

* 20 FOOT WINDS...Southwest 15 to 20 mph with gusts up to 35 mph.

* RELATIVE HUMIDITY...As low as 20 percent.

* 10 HOUR FUELS...Less than 9 percent.

* IMPACTS...Any fire that develops will catch and spread
quickly. Outdoor burning is not recommended.
properties.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.

NWSheadline: RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 PM CST THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: Jefferson; Wayne; Perry; Franklin; Jackson; Williamson; Union; Alexander; Perry; Bollinger; Cape Girardeau; Wayne; Carter; Ripley; Butler; Stoddard; Scott; Mississippi; New Madrid
properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued February 27 at 1:21PM CST by NWS Paducah KY
properties.description:
Relative humidity values will fall to 20 to 30 percent by late
this afternoon. Southwest winds will be modest at 10 to 15 mph.
Given the very dry conditions across the region there will
continue to be an elevated risk for wildfire or brushfire growth
and spread for any fires that get started. Exercise extra caution
today in avoiding actions that could lead to fire starts.

NWSheadline: Elevated conditions for wildfire growth and spread through this afternoon

this Red Flag Warning.

NWSheadline: RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 PM CST THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: Muscatine; Scott; Louisa; Jefferson; Henry; Des Moines; Van Buren; Lee; Whiteside; Rock Island; Henry; Bureau; Putnam; Mercer; Henderson; Warren; Hancock; McDonough; Scotland; Clark
properties.headline: Red Flag Warning issued February 27 at 10:10AM CST until February 27 at 6:00PM CST by NWS Quad Cities IA IL
properties.description:
The National Weather Service in Quad Cities has issued a Red Flag
Warning for unseasonably mild temperatures, gusty winds, low
relative humidity, and plentiful dry vegetation, which is in
effect from noon today to 6 PM CST this evening.

* AFFECTED AREA...Portions of southeastern Iowa, western
Illinois, and northern Missouri.

* TIMING...From noon today to 6 PM CST this evening.

* WINDS...Southwest to west 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 40 mph.

* RELATIVE HUMIDITY...As low as 20 percent.

* TEMPERATURES...Middle to upper 60s.

* IMPACTS...Any fire that develops will catch and spread
quickly. Outdoor burning is not recommended.
properties.instruction:
A Red Flag Warning means that critical fire weather conditions
are either occurring now, or will shortly. A combination of
strong winds, low relative humidity, and warm temperatures can
contribute to extreme fire behavior.

NWSheadline: RED FLAG WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 PM CST THIS EVENING FOR CRITICAL FIRE DANGER

properties.areaDesc: Dubuque; Benton; Linn; Jones; Jackson; Iowa; Johnson; Cedar; Clinton; Keokuk; Washington; Jo Daviess; Stephenson; Carroll
properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued February 27 at 10:01AM CST by NWS Quad Cities IA IL
properties.description:
Strong westerly winds gusting up to 35 MPH will combine with
mild temperatures, low humidity, and plentiful dried vegetation
to produce elevated fire weather conditions today. If a fire were
to start under these conditions, there would be an increased
threat of fire spread and be difficult to control.

Outdoor burning is strongly discouraged. Use extra caution while
operating outdoor equipment around dry vegetation, and do not
carelessly discard cigarettes.

NWSheadline: Elevated Fire Weather Conditions This Afternoon

properties.areaDesc: Lee; De Kalb; Kane; DuPage; La Salle; Kendall; Grundy; Kankakee; Livingston; Iroquois; Ford; Northern Cook; Central Cook; Southern Cook; Northern Will; Southern Will; Eastern Will
properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued February 27 at 9:40AM CST by NWS Chicago IL
properties.description:
The combination of warm temperatures in the low to mid 60s,
relative humidity values dropping to the 25 to 35 percent range,
and southwest winds gusting 30 to 35 mph will lead to a threat for
brush and grass fire spread this afternoon.

Limit outdoor activities that involve open flame or sparks,
including operating farm equipment, parking on grass, and
improperly discarding smoking materials.

properties.areaDesc: Adams; Brown; Pike; Knox; Lewis; Shelby; Marion; Monroe; Ralls; Pike; Audrain
properties.headline: The Fire Weather Watch has been replaced. Please see the latest information from NWS St Louis MO on this developing situation.
properties.description: The Fire Weather Watch has been replaced. Please see the latest information from NWS St Louis MO on this developing situation.
properties.instruction:
Please advise the appropriate officials or fire crews in the field of
this Red Flag Warning.

NWSheadline: RED FLAG WARNING IN EFFECT FROM NOON TODAY TO 6 PM CST THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: Coastal Del Norte; Northern Humboldt Coast; Southwestern Humboldt
properties.headline: Beach Hazards Statement issued February 27 at 1:45PM PST until February 27 at 11:00PM PST by NWS Eureka CA
properties.description:
* WHAT...Increased threat of sneaker waves expected.

* WHERE...Coastal Del Norte, Northern Humboldt Coast and
Southwestern Humboldt Counties.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PST this evening. .

* IMPACTS...Large, unexpected waves can sweep across the beach
without warning, sweeping people into the sea from rocks,
jetties, and beaches. These sneaker waves can also move large
objects such as logs, crushing anyone caught underneath.
properties.instruction:
Don't be fooled by an ocean that looks calm. There can be 30
minutes of small waves before a sneaker wave strikes. Avoid rocks
and jetties. Avoid steep beaches. Stay much farther back from the
water and never turn your back on the ocean.

NWSheadline: BEACH HAZARDS STATEMENT REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 11 PM PST THIS EVENING

properties.areaDesc: San Luis Obispo County Beaches; San Luis Obispo County Inland Central Coast; Santa Lucia Mountains
properties.headline: Wind Advisory issued February 27 at 1:28PM PST until February 27 at 5:00PM PST by NWS Los Angeles/Oxnard CA
properties.description:
* WHAT...Northeast winds 15 to 25 mph with gusts to 35 mph. Local
gusts to 45 mph across high elevations.

* WHERE...San Luis Obispo County Beaches, San Luis Obispo County
Inland Central Coast, and Santa Lucia Mountains.

* WHEN...Until 5 PM PST this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
properties.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

Secure outdoor objects.

NWSheadline: WIND ADVISORY NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 5 PM PST THIS AFTERNOON

properties.areaDesc: West Side of Fresno and Kings Counties; Caruthers - San Joaquin - Selma; Fresno-Clovis; Buttonwillow - Lost Hills - I5; Delano-Wasco-Shafter; Hanford - Corcoran - Lemoore; Visalia - Porterville - Reedley; Southeast San Joaquin Valley
properties.headline: Dense Fog Advisory issued February 27 at 12:57PM PST until February 28 at 10:00AM PST by NWS Hanford CA
properties.description:
* WHAT...Visibility one quarter mile or less in patchy dense fog.

* WHERE...Buttonwillow - Lost Hills - I5, Caruthers - San Joaquin -
Selma, Delano-Wasco-Shafter, Fresno-Clovis, Hanford - Corcoran -
Lemoore, Southeast San Joaquin Valley, Visalia - Porterville -
Reedley, and West Side of Fresno and Kings Counties.

* WHEN...From 2 AM to 10 AM PST Saturday.

* IMPACTS...Low visibility could make driving conditions hazardous.
properties.instruction:
If driving, slow down, use your headlights, and leave plenty of
distance ahead of you as sudden reductions in visibility will occur.

NWSheadline: DENSE FOG ADVISORY IN EFFECT FROM 2 AM TO 10 AM PST SATURDAY
